title: Making a folio wallet
materials: bookbinding-board-2mm (two pieces for front and back)
sources: Folio/portfolio case structures documented in Arthur Johnson, The Thames and Hudson Manual of Bookbinding (1978, copyright, structural description only). The basic case-with-spine form is in Cockerell, Bookbinding and the Care of Books (1901).
notes: A hard-sided folio case for storing loose A4 or A5 sheets, drawings, or prints. Consists of two boards connected by a cloth-covered spine, so the whole thing lies flat when open and closes to a clamshell-like form. An elastic loop holds it closed. Covers: calculating spine width from the stack of sheets it will hold (add 3-5 mm for comfort), cutting the boards, cloth-covering with mitred corners, lining the inside faces, attaching the spine strip, punching and threading the elastic closure. The folio wallet has no book pages — it is a box-like cover only.
